steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both authors criticize the scope of current modernist scholarship. Text 1 argues scholars overrepresent fragmented narratives and overlook coherent ones like von Armin’s. Text 2 argues that fixating on “fragmentation versus coherence” inhibits inquiry into other stylistic elements. Both share the view that the field’s current focus is too narrow and produces a limited picture of the period.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘the vision it presents will continue to be unnecessarily limited’: matches both texts. Text 1 shows von Armin stays overlooked when scholars spotlight fragmented narratives. Text 2 states the narrow focus “inhibits inquiry” into other stylistic elements. Both predict or warn that the field remains limited without broader focus. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘instead of engaging in unproductive debates’: neither author frames the current state as a debate to be avoided. Both critique the field’s narrow scope, not the quality of its argumentation. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘its primary methods are growing obsolete’: Text 2 presents distant reading as a tool to expand inquiry, not as evidence that current methods are obsolete. Text 1 says nothing about methodology becoming outdated. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘widen its focus to include aspects beyond style’: both authors focus specifically on stylistic representation. Neither calls for moving beyond style — they want broader attention within style. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
